codeReview.summary: Created three reusable validation bootstrap assets (seed data JSON, application script, and library documentation) to enable frontend-decomposition workers to run dashboard validation against representative pipeline data. Implementation is clean and well-structured with proper error handling. Seed data matches the pipeline-row.v1.json schema correctly.

issues.description: Documentation states 'The discovery-local-bootstrap.json already points sheetId at this same disposable sheet' but discovery-local-bootstrap.json actually has sheetId: "" (empty). This is a factual discrepancy. The handoff makes the same claim. The sheet ID is correctly set in worker-config.json and seed-pipeline-data.json, but the discovery-local-bootstrap.json reference is inaccurate.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The seed-pipeline-data.json references a schema URL (https://github.com/job-bored/command-center/schemas/pipeline-row.v1.json) that may not exist at that GitHub location. The local schema file exists at schemas/pipeline-row.v1.json with matching $id, but if workers try to fetch the schema remotely for validation, it may fail. Consider either publishing the schema to GitHub or noting that local schema validation should be used.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: evidence/seed-pipeline-data.json:2 — $schema points to GitHub URL; schemas/pipeline-row.v1.json exists locally with matching $id

sharedStateObservations.observation: The apply-validation-bootstrap.mjs script follows a good pattern for validation infrastructure scripts: read local data, verify structure, cross-check with related config files, and print actionable instructions. This pattern could be documented in AGENTS.md for future validation bootstrap scripts.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: scripts/apply-validation-bootstrap.mjs — structured with verification, cross-check with worker-config.json, and clear browser application instructions

summary: PASS with minor documentation accuracy issue. The feature successfully creates reusable bootstrap assets for frontend-decomposition validation. The seed data is well-formed with 8 rows covering all workflow stages, and the application script correctly verifies the data and provides clear instructions. The main issue is that the documentation inaccurately claims discovery-local-bootstrap.json has the sheetId set, when it's actually empty. This doesn't block the feature since workers can use the localStorage override or setup-browser-cookies skill as documented, but the handoff and environment.md should be corrected for accuracy.
